items.claim: Cody Gibson is a tall lanky guy for the division and uses his length really well with a good jab and good teeps.
items.quote: He's a good striker, uses his length really well. He's a tall lanky guy for the division and he uses it as such. Good jab, good teeps

items.claim: Cody Gibson gets backed up, which takes the sting off his shots and allows opponents to negate his range against the cage.
items.quote: the problem is he does get backed up and that can cost him a lot because when you're getting backed up and you're this tall lanky fellow, it takes a lot of the sting off your shots and also it gets them to back you up to the cage where they can negate some of that range.

items.claim: Cody Gibson has solid wrestling fundamentals and is very active from his back with scrambles and nasty elbows.
items.quote: He's a good grappler, too. He's got really solid wrestling fundamentals. He's a good back taker and even if he's on his back, he's very active from his back. Scrambles really well, nasty elbows in the ground and pound.

items.claim: Abdul Hussein has a solid choke arsenal and can snatch up various chokes from pretty much anywhere.
items.quote: he's got a solid choke arsenal. He can snatch up various chokes from pretty much anywhere.

items.claim: Mohammad Said can slow down, which can be a problem especially if he is forced to grapple.
items.quote: Now, he can slow down. That can be a problem for him, especially if he's forced to grapple.

items.claim: Dustin Jacoby has surprising power and a ton of knockouts.
items.quote: Dustin Jacoby's a solid striker in his own right. Really good volume... and surprising power. People always forget that Dustin Jacoby's powerful because he doesn't look like a power puncher, but the dude has a ton of knockouts.

items.claim: Dustin Jacoby has good takedown defense and immediately goes into striking upon stuffing a takedown.
items.quote: He's also got good takedown defense and as soon as he stuffs a takedown, he goes right into striking which makes it really hard for people who want to continually shoot takedowns.

items.claim: Sam Patterson has tall man defense and tends to leave his chin up to be hit.
items.quote: he does have that tall man defense. And if Sam Patterson loses this fight that's probably why. Because he does just kind of have his chin up there to be hit.

items.claim: Sam Patterson has good cardio despite being a bigger fighter for the division.
items.quote: He's also got pretty good cardio. You don't usually think of a guy that is kind of like bigger for the division to have good cardio.

items.claim: Ever since Santiago Ponzinibbio had a big injury a few years ago, he never quite came back the same.
items.quote: ever since Ponzi had that big injury like few years ago... he never quite came back the same.

items.claim: Mike Davis has pretty good takedown defense in a sprawl traditionally, aside from one fight affected by paint fumes.
items.quote: typically he's got pretty good takedown defense in a sprawl. There was the fight where he said the paint fumes got to him or whatever... traditionally he's got pretty good takedowns or takedown defense, sorry.

items.claim: Nurullo Aliev's striking is set up by his grappling threat rather than high-level technical striking ability.
items.quote: For Aliev, decent striker. It's basically the grappling threat sets it up and it sets up his flurries... he's not a good striker by any means

items.claim: Nurullo Aliev has good pressure on top once he gets the fight to the mat, smothering opponents and trapping arms.
items.quote: Once he gets on top, his pressure is pretty darn good and he smothers you. Eventually, he'll trap your arms and start landing some ground and pound
